title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Terminology
2-Step Security
2-step security is a type of step-up authentication that is added to an application. To log in to an application that is protected by 2-step security, a user must enter the one-time password from their registered 2-step security token.
2-Step Security Token
A 2-step security token is a device or application that generates a random one-time password that is recognized by applications that are protected by 2-step security.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1芒聙聶s applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users can芒聙聶t manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications.
Realm
A realm is a setting on CPS Admin that controls which Client Centre Users a CPS Admin Security Officer can manage. If a CPS Admin Security Officer isn芒聙聶t assigned to a realm, they can manage all the financial institution芒聙聶s Client Centre Users whether or not a Client Centre User has a realm. If a CPS Admin Security Officer is assigned to a realm, the CPS Admin Security Officer can only manage Client Centre Users set up with the same realm.
Step-up Authentication
Step-up authentication is any additional identity verification (e.g., a 2-step security token) required to validate a user芒聙聶s access to a higher security level.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
General Client Centre and CPS Admin Questions
Question 1
Should I use CPS Admin or the User Management application to manage users?
Answer
You can use either application but are encouraged to use the User Management application. You are limited to managing Client Centre Users via CPS Admin, but you can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers via User Management. The only changes you can芒聙聶t make to other CPS Admins are changing the realm (that芒聙聶s only available on CPS Admin), resetting passwords, and assigning administrative rights. Also, only Central聽1 can create CPS Admin Security Officers.
Question 2
What is the difference between the Client Centre and the Central Portal System (CPS) Admin?
Answer
The Client Centre is the single entry point for users to securely access online content, services, and applications offered by Central聽1.
To access the Client Centre (including applications such as ORS, Wires, OLT, etc.), you must have a username that is linked to the appropriate applications.
CPS Admin is a user management application for the Client Centre that allows CPS Admin Security Officers to manage Client Centre Users.
Question 3
Do I need access to CPS Admin?
Answer
Unless you are a CPS Admin Security Officer, you don芒聙聶t need access to CPS Admin.
If you are a CPS Admin Security Officer, you can use CPS Admin for user management purposes (i.e., to add, modify, or delete Client Centre Users) but are encouraged to use the User Management application.
Question 4
Who are my CPS Admin Security Officers?
Answer
Contact Client Support Services
if you need help identifying your CPS Admin Security Officers. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites.
Set-up and Registration Questions
Question 5
I芒聙聶m new and require access to the Client Centre. I芒聙聶m not a CPS Admin Security Officer. What should I do?
Answer
Ask your CPS Admin Security Officer to set up a username for you. Follow your internal processes. This will give you access to the Client Centre.
Question 6
I芒聙聶ve been appointed as a CPS Admin Security Officer, and I require access to the Client Centre and CPS Admin. What should I do?
Answer
Complete the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, and send it to your provincial central credit union. Indicate whether you need
personal access to use specific applications and/or,
administrative rights to grant other users access to specific applications.
Some applications are associated with 芒聙聹parameters芒聙聺 (i.e., additional user management settings such as a branch number, user type, etc.). You must include all parameters on Form 1918. Generally, you can find information on what parameters are required for an application by referring to the applicable Part in the Payment Solutions Manual. Alternatively, refer to the consolidated list of applications in the User Management Part.
Question 7
When I register my password, should I go to CPS Admin or to the Client Centre?
Answer
To register passwords, all users go to the Client Centre

(
https://clients.central1.com
/).You will sign in with your username and one-time password and then change your password at the prompt. This process automatically registers your password.
Question 8
There is an application on the Client Centre that I need to access but I can芒聙聶t see it. I芒聙聶m not a CPS Admin Security Officer and I already have access to the Client Centre. What should I do?
Answer
Ask your CPS Admin Security Officer to provide you with access to the application. Follow your internal processes.
Question 9
I芒聙聶m a CPS Admin Security Officer and want to access an application on the Client Centre but I can芒聙聶t see it. Also, I can芒聙聶t grant a user access to the application. What should I do?
Answer
Complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, and send it to your provincial central credit union. For the application in question, indicate whether you need:
personal access to use the application and/or
administrative rights to grant other users access to the application.
Some applications are associated with 芒聙聹parameters芒聙聺 (i.e., additional user management settings such as a branch number, user type, etc.). You must include all parameters on Form 2035. Generally, you can find information on what parameters are required for an application by referring to the applicable Part in the Payment Solutions Manual. Alternatively, refer to the consolidated list of applications in the User Management Part.
If you only require personal access to an application, not administrative rights, you can have another CPS Admin Security Officer assign the application to you via the User Management application.
Login Questions
Question 10
I can芒聙聶t log in to the Client Centre (or CPS Admin). What should I do?
Answer
Ensure that you use the correct username and enter the entire username (e.g., don芒聙聶t enter 12345 instead of CU12345).
If you share a computer with another user, close all browsers, restart the browser, and try again.
Question 11
When I log in to the Client Centre and access an application, the application doesn芒聙聶t open, and I receive an error message. What should I do?
Answer
If you share a computer with another user, it is possible that the other user didn芒聙聶t log out properly.
Log out of the application using the 芒聙聹Logout芒聙聺 button and not the 芒聙聹X芒聙聺. Wait until the application states that you have successfully logged out and then log out of the Client Centre using the 芒聙聹Logout芒聙聺 button.
Once the Client Centre has successfully logged you out, close all browsers. Restart the browser, log in to the Client Centre, and then access the application.
END
